vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-619: Cost tracking: comma-format total cost, add day/week ticks to 30-day trend

plan.title: Cost tracking: comma-format total cost, add day/week ticks to 30-day trend
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: Large dollar values display without thousands separators (e.g. $1234.56), and the 30-day trend chart lacks time-axis ticks making it hard to identify when cost spikes occurred.
narratives.Proposal: Add a formatCost helper using toLocaleString for comma-separated dollar values across all displays, and configure Chart.js x-axis with daily tick marks and weekly date labels.

items.title: Comma-format all dollar values in CostsPage

narrative.Action: Add a formatCost(value, decimals) helper that uses toLocaleString('en-US', { minimumFractionDigits, maximumFractionDigits }) and replace all .toFixed() dollar displays in CostsPage.tsx with it. Use 2 decimals for summary/list values and 4 decimals for sub-cent precision values.

subItems.title: Total cost card displays with comma separators (e.g. $1,234.56)
Per-issue costs and budget values in the issues list display with comma separators
Sub-cent precision values (.toFixed(4)) also use locale formatting
Chart Y-axis and tooltip values use locale formatting

items.title: Add day/week tick marks to 30-day trend chart x-axis

narrative.Action: Configure the Chart.js x-axis in TrendChart to show tick marks for every day with date labels only on week boundaries (every 7th day from the end). Set autoSkip: false and use a ticks.callback that returns the MM-DD label for week boundaries and empty string otherwise.

subItems.title: X-axis shows tick marks for all 30 days
Date labels appear at week boundaries (every 7 days) for readability
No regression to chart rendering or other cost displays
